title: Gouldia crassicaulis
taxonomy: Gouldia crassicaulis is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrsinaceae. It is native to the island of Kahoolawe in Hawaii, USA. The classification of Gouldia crassicaulis includes its kingdom as Plantae, phylum as Magnoliophyta, class as Magnoliopsida, and order as Ericales. This plant is also known as the Kahoolawe silverwood. Gouldia crassicaulis is considered an endangered species due to habitat loss and other environmental factors.
